Project2 Data Mining

Group 6: Cristhian Cruz, Julian McKinley, Luisa Ribeiro, Matheus Lucena

2026-03-03

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(caret)
  library(MASS)
  library(leaps)
  library(glmnet)
  library(dplyr)
})

set.seed(5703)
df_raw <- read.csv("kc_house_data.csv")
# Clean Types + Drop ID/Date From Modeling
df <- df_raw

# Convert likely categorical predictors
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    zipcode = if ("zipcode" %in% names(.)) as.factor(zipcode) else NULL,
    waterfront = if ("waterfront" %in% names(.)) as.factor(waterfront) else NULL,
    view = if ("view" %in% names(.)) as.factor(view) else NULL,
    condition = if ("condition" %in% names(.)) as.factor(condition) else NULL,
    grade = if ("grade" %in% names(.)) as.factor(grade) else NULL
  )

# Create modeling dataset
drop_cols <- intersect(names(df), c("id", "date"))
df_model <- dplyr::select(df, -dplyr::all_of(drop_cols))

# We will be using log(price) for final model
df_model <- df_model %>% mutate(log_price = log(price))

stopifnot("price" %in% names(df_model))
# Train/Test Split
set.seed(5703)
idx <- createDataPartition(df_model$log_price, p = 0.80, list = FALSE)
train <- df_model[idx, , drop = FALSE]
test  <- df_model[-idx, , drop = FALSE]

cat("Train rows:", nrow(train), "\nTest rows:", nrow(test), "\n")
## Train rows: 17292 
## Test rows: 4321

Part 1: Model selection methods

A. Best Subset Selection

p <- length(setdiff(names(train), c("price","log_price","zipcode")))

best_fit <- regsubsets(log_price ~ . -price - zipcode, data = train, nvmax = p)
## Warning in leaps.setup(x, y, wt = wt, nbest = nbest, nvmax = nvmax, force.in =
## force.in, : 1 linear dependencies found
## Reordering variables and trying again:
best_summary <-summary(best_fit)

plot(best_summary$adjr2, type="b", main="Adjusted R2")

plot(best_summary$cp, type="b", main="Cp")

plot(best_summary$bic, type="b", main="BIC")

B. Stepwise Selection(Forward, Backward or Both)

full_model <- lm(log_price ~ . -price, data = train)

step_model <- step(full_model, direction = "both", trace = FALSE)
summary(step_model)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = log_price ~ bathrooms + sqft_living + sqft_lot + 
##     floors + waterfront + view + condition + grade + sqft_above + 
##     yr_built + yr_renovated + zipcode + lat + long + sqft_living15, 
##     data = train)
##

## F-statistic:  1293 on 99 and 17192 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
#top 10 significant predictors
top_vars <- broom::tidy(step_model) %>%
  filter(p.value < 0.01) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(statistic))) %>%
  head(10)

top_vars
## # A tibble: 10 × 5

Part 2: Regularization and 5-fold cross validation

our outcome is continuous so we will be using ridge and lasso

# exclude price and log_price from predictors
x_train <- model.matrix(log_price ~ . - price - log_price, data = train)[, -1]
y_train <- train$log_price

x_test <- model.matrix(log_price ~ . - price - log_price, data = test)[, -1]
y_test <- test$log_price

# 5-fold CV for Ridge Regression
set.seed(5703)
cv_ridge <- cv.glmnet(x_train, y_train, alpha = 0, nfolds = 5)
plot(cv_ridge)
title("Ridge Regression CV Error Curve")

# Optimal lambdas
cv_ridge$lambda.min
## [1] 0.03703292
cv_ridge$lambda.1se
## [1] 0.06471607
# Ridge coefficients at lambda.min
coef(cv_ridge, s = "lambda.min")
## 103 x 1 sparse Matrix of class "dgCMatrix"

# 5-fold CV for LASSO Regression
set.seed(5703)
cv_lasso <- cv.glmnet(x_train, y_train, alpha = 1, nfolds = 5)
plot(cv_lasso)
title("LASSO Regression CV Error Curve")

# Optimal lambdas
cv_lasso$lambda.min
## [1] 8.555084e-05
cv_lasso$lambda.1se
## [1] 0.002022841
# LASSO coefficients at lambda.min
lasso_coef <- coef(cv_lasso, s = "lambda.min")
lasso_coef
